message:
perf(core): record MemDatabase row clone rollback [bd-1dp9.6.7.11]

The symbolized pre-A1 flamegraph kept SqliteValue slice-to-Vec cloning visible at 52,223,806 samples (1.06%), but the Arc<[SqliteValue]> MemRowValues patch regressed the target workload instead of improving it.

Hyperfine, same machine, /tmp/cargo-target/release-perf/perf-update-delete 10000 10 both: pre/reverted mean 264.6 ms +/- 3.9 ms; patched mean 271.5 ms +/- 4.5 ms. Cargo check for the patched tree passed with rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe -p fsqlite-core --all-targets, but the benchmark result was below the ship threshold, so the code change was rolled back and this conclusion artifact is committed instead.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# PERF-A1 MemDatabase Row Values Conclusion
+
+Bead `bd-1dp9.6.7.11`: the pre-A1 symbolized flamegraph still showed `SqliteValue` slice-to-Vec cloning as visible (`<fsqlite_types::value::SqliteValue as <[_]>::to_vec_in::ConvertVec>::to_vec`, 52,223,806 samples, 1.06%), with surrounding `MemTable`/`MemDatabase` clone and insert frames, so the hotspot was real; however the least-invasive `Arc<[SqliteValue]>` row-value patch compiled (`rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe -p fsqlite-core --all-targets`) but regressed the target benchmark on the same machine: reverted pre-change `hyperfine --warmup 1 --runs 7 --show-output '/tmp/cargo-target/release-perf/perf-update-delete 10000 10 both'` reported `264.6 ms +/- 3.9 ms`, while the patched build reported `271.5 ms +/- 4.5 ms`, so the API/container churn was rolled back and should not ship as A1.
